Because of the leadership of the Pacific Islands, big countries and small states alike are better positioned to:
✔️choose their own path
✔️enjoy greater access to clean air and water
✔️protect more of the planet for their children and grandchildren https://t.co/sVW1S5UXwn

In “The Week at State” with @GPA_AS, @POTUS hosts the U.S.-Pacific Islands Forum Summit, former Secretary @HillaryClinton returns for her official portrait unveiling, and @SecBlinken launches the Global Music Diplomacy Initiative. Head over to https://t.co/58Uc9lIOt7 for more. https://t.co/ykJAi1ltAp

In 1963 as pioneers of #MusicDiplomacy and one of the original jazz ambassadors, Duke Ellington and his orchestra traveled to India to play a concert. Now, 60 years later, the next generation of artists continue to be moved and impacted by their music. 🎷 🎹 🎺 https://t.co/KZ0iDHr2OX

Today we are launching the U.S. Department of State’s Global Music Diplomacy Initiative to elevate music as a diplomatic tool to promote peace and democracy. https://t.co/ajM7wYYuiQ https://t.co/GeniihXaMe

Russia alone bears responsibility for this war. Russia alone has the power to end this war immediately. And it is Russia alone that stands in the way of peace, because Russia’s price for peace is Ukraine’s capitulation, Ukraine’s territory, and Ukraine’s children. https://t.co/LNqDkZSVLu
Former Secretary @HillaryClinton's portrait joins the official portraits of all other former secretaries of state. Your legacy continues.

The world has changed, yet the core of the Department’s work remains. @HillaryClinton’s parting words as Secretary ring true. We continue to work with all our heart and might to make sure that America is secure, that our interests are promoted, and our values are respected. https://t.co/Ta7PD4ov8v

In “The Week at State,” @USUNSpox recaps #UNGA78, including @POTUS’ address to the @UN General Assembly and engagements held by @SecBlinken, @USAmbUN, and other top officials. The United States, together with our allies and partners around the world, will continue to stand with the brave people of Ukraine as they defend their sovereignty and territorial integrity and their freedom. https://t.co/0BWvM3gK6V
To effectively protect our people, we need to work together, which is why we launched the Global Coalition to Address Synthetic Drug Threats. https://t.co/XdYRoHcKQQ https://t.co/PVHKe5Ujsm

In the United States, synthetic drugs are the number-one killer of Americans aged 18 to 49. It has devastated families. It has devastated communities. It’s also been overwhelming to our public health and criminal justice systems. https://t.co/2NLEEa5u9n

The ocean is one global system, and its health is key to the health of our planet. Together with our partners around the world, the United States is committed to the conservation and sustainable use of the high seas and its resources. https://t.co/Eb8wyMTcz8

This week at the @UN, the U.S. proudly joined other signatories in signing the new High Seas Treaty, a commitment to conservation and sustainable use of marine biodiversity in our shared waters. More on what this means for you. https://t.co/hK8Mbx1WGi https://t.co/RUysRopLXp

UNGA is a special week for New York City and Under Secretary Jose Fernandez. Hear from @State_E as he shares how his hometown changes during #UNGA78 and the work we’re doing to protect our oceans. https://t.co/jZgk9rgEgG

The Sustainable Development Goals are 17 goals that call for global action by all countries to end poverty, protect the planet, and ensure peace. #UNGA78 https://t.co/oogklwlQFW https://t.co/b6pUy74Eki

On the International Day of Sign Languages, we honor visual forms of communication and recognize all who are making the world more accessible through learning and using sign languages. We are committed to advocating for the deaf community’s rights to inclusion and access. https://t.co/4GckDep4Ww

The United States is working with our partners to make global institutions more responsive, more effective, and more inclusive. And we embrace nations stepping up to lead in new ways and to seek new breakthroughs on hard issues. https://t.co/yOc8rUag3l
The United States proudly supports Ukraine in its fight to protect its country and people. No one can erase Ukraine’s history or culture. #UnitedwithUkraine

Interpreters play a vital role at the @un, ensuring even the most nuanced language is interpreted correctly for world leaders and top diplomats. Our Director of the Office of Language Services explains how her team supports high-level diplomacy each September. https://t.co/AEvg0IeVqy
The United States is building global coalitions to address shared challenges—from food insecurity and emerging technologies to artificial intelligence, and synthetic drugs. https://t.co/5fGR4fr2b8

Congrats to the @_AfricanUnion on joining the G20. The U.S. will continue to work with the African Union and African leaders and partners to create opportunities and meet the challenges faced by the region. https://t.co/VeWPcj6hWh

On this International Day of Democracy, we are pleased to announce the Lorne Craner Democracy Fellowship. Fellows will be invaluable members of the @StateDRL team in responding to new and emerging threats to democracy globally. https://t.co/qeJHm58rd1
As Rosh Hashanah begins, we send best wishes to all celebrating the New Year. May the New Year be filled with health, happiness, and progress. https://t.co/ChWS4zLLrb

Curious about what “UNGA” means and why it matters? @GPA_AS Russo explains what @POTUS, @SecBlinken, and other U.S. leaders will do in New York next week — and our work to turn today’s shared challenges into tomorrow’s shared opportunities. https://t.co/gBBMyYkTM6

Today we honor the memory of Mahsa Zhina Amini of Iran, who died in the custody of the regime’s so-called “morality police.” As the epitaph on her grave reads: Her name will become a symbol. It has. And it will always be a reminder of the courage of the Iranian people. https://t.co/FmBmVzi4MV
Every single day Russian bombs, Russian rockets, Russian drones are raining down upon the people of Ukraine, tearing apart innocent lives. https://t.co/MYBjKVpkax

International Day of Democracy reminds us that democracies are stronger when they are inclusive and when every voice matters. The United States is committed to protecting democratic principles and supporting the civil and political rights of all people. https://t.co/v7tdu1gDam

Today marks the beginning of #HispanicHeritageMonth, when we recognize the vital role that Latin and Hispanic-Americans play in our communities across the U.S. This month we celebrate the many leaders who continue to make our nation more equitable, secure, and prosperous. https://t.co/XbRsZrFPXn

At @SAISHopkins, @SecBlinken outlined the power and purpose of U.S. diplomacy. Our vision for the future is a world that is open, free, prosperous, and secure – both for the American people and for people around the world. https://t.co/gTFJLUtcjl

.@SecBlinken: We’re upgrading infrastructure, boosting research, bolstering the key industries and technologies of the 21st century, recharging our manufacturing base, and leading the global energy transition. https://t.co/x3XQWcbQma

.@SecBlinken: Russia’s war of aggression in Ukraine is the most immediate, the most acute threat to the international order enshrined in the UN Charter and its core principles of sovereignty, territorial integrity, and independence. https://t.co/UZbD9XQy9b
.@SecBlinken: We are bringing our old and new coalitions together to strengthen the international institutions that are vital to tackling global challenges. https://t.co/TdBUaQ954x
Tune into @SecBlinken conversation on U.S. foreign policy with Tom Vietor and Ben Rhodes on Pod Save the World’s latest episode: https://t.co/wThlE4CBQi

.@SecBlinken discusses sitting down with leaders in countries to tackle the food security crisis. "What they really want is investment in agricultural resilience and innovation in self sufficiency so that they don't find themselves in a crisis like this." https://t.co/QIKB6wLcdh

.@SecBlinken: We’re working with purpose and urgency to deepen, broaden, and align our friends in new ways so that we can meet the three defining tests of this emerging era: a fierce and lasting strategic competition. https://t.co/0Uqb0wweyd

.@SecBlinken on President Putin’s war: Our goal is to ensure Ukraine not only survives but thrives as a vibrant, prosperous democracy — so that Ukrainians can write their own future. https://t.co/QOO5F1Tuuv

Live now! @SecBlinken participates in a Security Integration and Prosperity Agreement signing ceremony with Bahraini Crown Prince and Prime Minister Salman bin Hamad Al Khalifa. https://t.co/PbKCMHlSAo

.@SecBlinken: Our vision for the future, a world that is open, free, prosperous and secure, that vision is not America's alone, but the enduring aspiration of people in every nation on every continent. https://t.co/XowaIjUSQw

.@SecBlinken: We must put our hand on the rudder of history and chart a path forward, guided by the things that are certain, even in uncertain times, our principals, our partners, our vision for where we want to go. So that, when the fog lifts, the world that emerges tilts toward freedom, toward peace, toward an international community capable of rising to the challenges of this time.

.@SecBlinken: It’s no surprise that counties would prefer investments that foster more transparent, high-quality & environmentally sustainable projects.

With G7 partners, we’ve committed to deliver $600 billion by 2027 toward projects through the Partnership for Global Infrastructure & Investment.

.@SecBlinken: We are determined to work with any country – including those with whom we disagree on important issues – so long as they want to deliver for their citizens, contribute to solving shared challenges, and uphold the shared international norms.

.@SecBlinken at @SAISHopkins: Our vision for the future – a world that is free, open, prosperous, and secure – is not America’s alone, but the enduring aspiration of people in every nation, on every continent.
Live now! @SecBlinken delivers a speech at @SAISHopkins as part of the Brzezinski Lecture Series. Also streaming at: https://t.co/dx4bgqrB8v  https://t.co/Q2Y0c3r2iw

As Russia continues to attack Ukraine’s ports and silos, nearby countries are stepping up efforts to deliver more of Ukraine’s grain to the world’s markets and to people who need it most — including in Yemen, Ethiopia, Somalia, and Afghanistan. Learn more: https://t.co/Yd46ibv5N3

Russia’s attacks on Ukrainian grain silos are devastating for farmers in Ukraine — and for starving people around the world.
So many families can be fed by this vital lifeline if Russia stops its cruel attacks and rejoins the Black Sea Grain Initiative. https://t.co/4GyOYAxy4O
The Black Sea Grain Initiative delivered over 30 million tons of Ukrainian food products to the world, with two-thirds of the wheat going to developing countries. https://t.co/81PuUp8G88
